1.0.title: INTEGRATE CAREER AND TECHNICAL STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS (CTSOs)

1.1.title: Explore the History and Organization of CTSOs

1.1.1.description: Discuss the requirements of CTSO participation/involvement as described in Carl D. Perkins Law

1.1.3.description: Investigate the impact of federal and state government regarding the progression and operation of CTSOs (e.g., Federal Statutes and Regulations, Nevada Administrative Code [NAC], Nevada Revised Statutes [NRS])

1.2.1.description: Discuss the purpose of parliamentary procedure

1.2.2.description: Demonstrate the proper use of parliamentary procedure

1.2.3.description: Differentiate between an office and a committee

1.2.4.description: Discuss the importance of participation in local, regional, state, and national conferences, events, and competitions

1.2.5.description: Participate in local, regional, state, or national conferences, events, or competitions

1.2.6.description: Describe the importance of a constitution and bylaws to the operation of a CTSO chapter

1.3.1.description: Explore opportunities in community service-related work-based learning (WBL)

1.3.2.description: Participate in a service learning (program related) and/or community service project or activity

1.3.3.description: Engage with business and industry partners for community service

1.4.1.description: Demonstrate college and career readiness (e.g., applications, resumes, interview skills, presentation skills)

1.4.2.description: Describe the appropriate professional/workplace attire and its importance

1.4.3.description: Investigate industry-standard credentials/certifications available within this Career Cluster™

1.4.4.description: Participate in authentic contextualized instructional activities

1.4.5.description: Demonstrate technical skills in various student organization activities/events

1.5.title: Understand the Relevance of Career and Technical Education (CTE)

1.5.1.description: Make a connection between program standards to career pathway(s)

1.5.2.description: Explain the importance of participation and completion of a program of study

1.5.3.description: Promote community awareness of local student organizations associated with CTE programs

2.0.title: FOUNDATIONS OF WEB DESIGN AND DEVELOPMENT

2.1.title: Understand the History of Web Design and Development

2.1.1.description: Describe the role of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) in defining web standards

2.1.2.description: Research the history of the World Wide Web

2.1.3.description: Compare and contrast the Internet and the World Wide Web

2.2.2.description: Explain and apply the principles of design

2.2.3.description: Explain and apply the elements of design

2.2.5.description: Evaluate the use of white space

2.3.1.description: Define common terminology and their acronyms

2.3.2.description: Differentiate between front-end and back-end development

2.3.3.description: Explain the various roles and careers related to web design

2.4.title: Describe the Relationship Between Social Media and Web

2.4.1.description: Describe the role of social media in web development

2.4.2.description: Explain the correlation between social media platforms and web links

2.4.3.description: Describe the relationship of advertising, marketing, social media, and websites

2.4.4.description: Discuss current trends in social media

2.4.5.description: Create and implement a strategy that uses social networks to drive traffic to a website

2.5.1.description: Define e-commerce as it relates to web development

2.5.2.description: Demonstrate how to integrate a shopping cart into a web page

2.5.4.description: Discuss security concerns as they relate to e-commerce

3.0.title: ETHICAL AND SECURE USE OF INFORMATION

3.1.title: Describe Copyright Laws in Relation to Web Development

3.1.1.description: Research laws that govern intellectual property in diverse forms

3.1.2.description: Evaluate Creative Commons licensing and other free-content license types

3.1.3.description: Cite the boundaries of third-party work

3.1.4.description: Explain terms related to copyright, trademarks, patents, and other intellectual property

3.2.title: Explain Security Issues in Relation to Web Development

3.2.1.description: Explain invasion of privacy in the use of technology

3.2.3.description: Analyze the implications of a personal digital footprint

3.2.4.description: Differentiate between secure and unsecure web protocols

3.2.5.description: Explain the implications of General Data Protection Regulations (GDPR)

3.2.6.description: Explain the implications of Nevada Consumer Privacy Act

3.2.7.description: Compare and contrast global privacy policies and cultural impacts

3.2.8.description: Describe how a security certificate protects a website

3.3.1.description: Model legal and ethical use of information

3.3.2.description: Describe the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement (NDA)

4.1.1.description: Describe the web design and development cycle

4.1.2.description: Demonstrate the web design and development cycle (e.g., analyze, design, develop, implement, evaluate)

4.2.1.description: Create a maintainable directory structure for a website

4.2.3.description: Describe the usage of correct file paths for relative and absolute links

4.2.4.description: Recognize the relationship between local and remote site structures

4.2.5.description: Develop a data backup procedures plan

4.3.1.description: Identify commonly used layout techniques for web design

4.3.2.description: Develop an appropriate navigation system (site map)

4.3.3.description: Develop wireframes for initial responsive design concepts

4.3.4.description: Develop responsive design for various devices

4.3.5.description: Identify the uses of Cascading Style Sheets (CSS)

4.4.1.description: Discuss and differentiate voice, tone, and style as it applies to web writing

4.4.2.description: Determine the primary and secondary purposes of web content

4.4.3.description: Identify target audiences and reading levels for specific websites

4.4.4.description: Identify and create a list of keywords and descriptions (meta tags) to include in web content for search engine optimization (SEO)

4.4.5.description: Apply grammar and spelling conventions to content

4.4.6.description: Evaluate existing content for web use (e.g., images, print documents, text, video)

4.4.7.description: Create a branding message that will present a professional image

4.4.8.description: List and describe best practices in content creation that foster indexing and ranking of websites

4.5.title: Create and Edit Media for the Web

4.5.2.description: Identify appropriate software for media creation

4.5.3.description: Create and edit media files (e.g., sound, video, graphics, interactive media, multimedia)

4.5.4.description: Optimize media files for uploading using compression tools

4.5.5.description: Embed media files in a web design

4.5.6.description: Calculate and convert images to desired sizes and resolution

4.6.title: Demonstrate Knowledge of Challenges Associated with

4.6.1.description: Describe regional, national, and international legal requirements and standards for accessibility on the web

4.6.2.description: Identify types of disabilities that should be considered when designing websites

4.6.3.description: Optimize websites to accommodate users with special needs

4.6.5.description: Research Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) compliance regulations and policies

5.1.title: Develop a Website Using Hypertext Markup Language (HTML)

5.1.1.description: Explain the purpose of Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) in website development

5.1.2.description: Differentiate among the different versions of HTML

5.1.3.description: Identify HTML tags for authoring a web page document

5.1.4.description: Code a basic web page utilizing proper HTML document structure in a text editor

5.1.5.description: Utilize verification tools to verify code

5.2.title: Describe Concepts and Use of Cascading Style Sheets

5.2.1.description: Describe the role of CSS in relation to web design

5.2.2.description: Identify the structure of CSS style rules

5.2.4.description: Differentiate between internal, external, and inline style sheets

5.2.5.description: Use CSS to style webpage content (e.g., typography, web fonts)

5.2.6.description: Use CSS to lay out webpage content (e.g., flexbox, grid, positioning: static, relative, absolute, fixed)

5.2.7.description: Utilize online validation tools for CSS

5.2.8.description: Describe the function of a CSS preprocessor

5.3.1.description: Explain the use of current web scripting technologies

5.3.3.description: Compare and contrast client (browser) scripting and server scripting (e.g., PHP, JavaScript)

5.3.4.description: Enhance interactivity of websites using current scripting trends

5.3.5.description: Compare and contrast static versus dynamic websites

5.3.6.description: Utilize online validation tools for web scripting

5.4.2.description: Analyze various databases used in web development

5.4.3.description: Describe the purpose of a database as it relates to web development

5.4.4.description: Incorporate a database into a website where possible (e.g., local storage, external database)

5.5.title: Utilize Content Management Systems in Web Development

5.5.1.description: Identify content management systems (CMS) (e.g., WordPress, Joomla)

5.5.2.description: Evaluate current trends in CMS (e.g., blogging, online magazine, corporate websites) and learning management system (LMS [e.g., Canvas])

5.5.3.description: Explore WYSIWYG software (e.g., Dreamweaver) for web development

5.6.2.description: Compare various cloud computing platforms (e.g., Microsoft Azure, Amazon AWS, Google Cloud)

5.6.3.description: Explain the role of version control when developing with a team

6.1.1.description: Identify the various server operating systems used to host web pages

6.1.2.description: Describe how servers work in a hosting environment

6.1.3.description: Explain the relationship between client and server

6.1.4.description: Explain common web server maintenance routines

6.1.5.description: Describe the technical requirements involved in choosing a web host

6.2.1.description: Identify the purpose of Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP)

6.2.3.description: Preview and test web pages for compatibility using various browsers and output devices

6.2.4.description: Describe the process of locating and registering a domain name

6.3.1.description: Evaluate content with client for relevancy

6.3.4.description: Maintain and update all website documentation (e.g., prototype, site map, navigation)

6.3.5.description: Analyze web analytics for purposes of improving traffic, user experience, and meeting targeted goals

7.0.title: ADVANCED AND EMERGING TECHNOLOGIES IN WEB DEVELOPMENT

7.1.1.description: Analyze current programming languages and/or platforms used in web app development

7.1.2.description: Explain the purpose of Application Programming Interface (API) in app development

7.1.3.description: Develop an app using a modern programming language and/or platform

7.2.2.description: Explain the role of AI in web development

7.2.3.description: Describe how AI changes the user experience

7.2.4.description: Explain the significance of data in the development of AI

7.3.1.description: Research Internet of Things (IoT) as it relates to web development

7.3.2.description: Explain the impact of digital assistants on web development

7.4.1.description: Explore use of VR and AR in web design

7.4.2.description: Research emerging applications of VR and AR in non-entertainment venues
